mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in real GDP signals expanding economic activity which increases corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 4-quarter correlation of GDP growth with subsequent 60-day SPX return is below 0.1, or directional accuracy under 55% over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4%, the labor market is considered extremely tight, prompting expectations of wage inflation and Fed tightening, which negatively impacts equities over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical episodes of UNRATE below 4% preceded 2000 and 2007 equity peaks
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in >55% of months when UNRATE is below 4%, or the signal's Sharpe ratio over rolling 1-year windows is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real interest rates, proxied by the federal funds rate minus CPI inflation, compress equity valuations by increasing the discount rate on future cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward rank IC of the real rate change versus 20-day SPX returns is below -0.03, or the strategy long SPX when real rates decline fails to beat buy-and-hold over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During recessionary periods signaled by industrial production falling for three consecutive months, equity markets anticipate an eventual recovery and generate positive forward returns over the following quarter as mean reversion sets in.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-recession equity rallies observed in 2001-2002 and 2008-2009
failure_condition: INDPRO recession signals are followed by negative or flat 60-day SPX returns in >50% of cases, or the average return post-signal is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals a housing market downturn, which propagates to consumer wealth and spending, dragging on corporate earnings and producing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Months with HOUST below 1200 show median SPX 20-day return above zero, or the signal's hit rate for negative returns is less than 60% over the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high unemployment and high inflation creates a stagflation environment where central bank policy is constrained, eroding real incomes and equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Stagflation episodes produce 60-day average SPX returns higher than non-stagflation periods, or the rank IC of the joint signal is positive over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or near zero (≤0.25%), the central bank's limited conventional easing capacity forces reliance on unconventional measures, which historically have supported equity prices through liquidity provision and portfolio rebalancing.
evidence_refs: fact:ZLB periods 2008-2015 and 2020-2021 saw positive equity trends
failure_condition: ZLB months produce negative median 20-day SPX returns, or the signal is not statistically distinguishable from non-ZLB periods (Mann-Whitney p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in industrial production indicate robust manufacturing activity and rising capacity utilization, which boosts real economic momentum and supports short-horizon equity gains over the next week.
failure_condition: The correlation between month-over-month INDPRO change and subsequent 5-day SPX return is negative over any 5-year rolling window, or the directional accuracy is below 50%.
